episode.name: API Error Burst
episode.description: Baseline traffic for five minutes, then GET /api/users starts returning ~40% errors (e.g. a bad release on the read path) for three minutes, then recovers.

segments.name: Baseline — healthy
segments.scenarioYaml:
- name: Web Service
- description: | 
    An AWS based web service composed of 3 EC2 instances. Two instances run a Node.js service. The services are connected to a load balancer. 
    the load balancer is connected to the internet. The third instance runs a MySQL database. The database is connected to the two Node.js services. 
    All instances are in the same subnet and in the same AWS VPC.
- nodes:
  - type: vpc
    name: Web Service VPC
    description: The AWS VPC for the web service.
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    cidr_block: 10.0.0.0/16
    tags:
      - Name: Web Service VPC
        Value: Web Service VPC
  - type: subnet
    name: Web Service Subnet
    description: The AWS subnet for the web service.
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    cidr_block: 10.0.1.0/24
    tags:
      - Name: Web Service Subnet
        Value: Web Service Subnet
  - type: virtual_server
    name: App Server 1
    description: EC2 instance for the app service
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    instance_type: t3.medium
    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.10
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: App Server 1
        Value: App Server 1
  - type: virtual_server
    name: App Server 2
    description: EC2 instance for the app service
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    instance_type: t3.medium
    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.11
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: App Server 2
        Value: App Server 2
  - type: virtual_server
    name: Database Server
    description: EC2 instance for the database
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    instance_type: t3.medium
    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.12
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: Database Server
        Value: Database Server
  - type: load_balancer
    name: Load Balancer
    description: The load balancer for the web service.
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    instance_type: t3.medium
    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.13
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: Load Balancer
        Value: Load Balancer
  - type: User Clients
    name: User Clients
    description: Group of user client making requests to the load balancer
    provider: null
    clients:
      - type: user_client
        name: Web Client 1
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36
        ip: 45.45.45.1
        rps: 1
        traffic_pattern: steady mix of GET and POST requests
      - type: user_client
        name: Web Client 2
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36
        ip: 45.45.45.2
        rps: 10
        traffic_pattern: only GET requests
      - type: user_client
        name: Mobile App Client 1
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 16_5 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/16.5 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1
        ip: 45.45.45.3
        rps: 0.1
        traffic_pattern: only POST requests
- services:
  - type: nodejs
    name: User Directory Service
    description: Node.js service running on App Server 1
    host: App Server 1
    generator:
      type: nodejs
      port: 3000
      log_format: json
      log_level: info
      endpoints:
        - method: GET
          path: /api/users
          avg_latency_ms: 100
          error_rate: 0.01
        - method: POST
          path: /api/users
          avg_latency_ms: 500
          error_rate: 0.01
  - type: mysql
    name: App Database
    description: MySQL database running on Database Server
    host: Database Server
    generator:
      type: mysql      
      port: 3306
      database: users
      slow_query_threshold: 1000
- connections:
  - source: User Clients
    target: Load Balancer
    protocol: https
    port: 443
  - source: Load Balancer
    target: User Directory Service
    protocol: http
    port: 3000
  - source: User Directory Service
    target: App Database
    protocol: mysql
    port: 3306

segments.name: Error burst on /api/users GET
segments.scenarioYaml:
- name: Web Service
- description: | 
    An AWS based web service composed of 3 EC2 instances. Two instances run a Node.js service. The services are connected to a load balancer.

    All instances are in the same subnet and in the same AWS VPC.

  - type: subnet

    cidr_block: 10.0.1.0/24

  - type: virtual_server

  - type: virtual_server

    os: ubuntu-22.04

    tags:
      - Name: App Server 2
        Value: App Server 2
  - type: virtual_server
    name: Database Server
    description: EC2 instance for the database
    provider: aws
    region: us-east-1
    instance_type: t3.medium
    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.12
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: Database Server
        Value: Database Server
  - type: load_balancer
    name: Load Balancer
    description: The load balancer for the web service.
    provider: aws

    os: ubuntu-22.04
    private_ip: 10.0.1.13
    security_groups:
      - sg-01234567890123456
    subnet: Web Service Subnet
    tags:
      - Name: Load Balancer
        Value: Load Balancer
  - type: User Clients
    name: User Clients
    description: Group of user client making requests to the load balancer
    provider: null
    clients:
      - type: user_client
        name: Web Client 1
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36
        ip: 45.45.45.1
        rps: 1
        traffic_pattern: steady mix of GET and POST requests
      - type: user_client
        name: Web Client 2
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36
        ip: 45.45.45.2
        rps: 10
        traffic_pattern: only GET requests
      - type: user_client
        name: Mobile App Client 1
        description: User client making requests to the load balancer
        user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 16_5 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/16.5 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1
        ip: 45.45.45.3
        rps: 0.1
        traffic_pattern: only POST requests
- services:
  - type: nodejs
    name: User Directory Service
    description: Node.js service running on App Server 1
    host: App Server 1
    generator:
      type: nodejs
      port: 3000
      log_format: json
      log_level: info
      endpoints:
        - method: GET
          path: /api/users
          avg_latency_ms: 100
          error_rate: 0.4
        - method: POST
          path: /api/users
          avg_latency_ms: 500
          error_rate: 0.01
  - type: mysql
    name: App Database
    description: MySQL database running on Database Server
    host: Database Server
    generator:
      type: mysql      
      port: 3306
      database: users
      slow_query_threshold: 1000
- connections:
  - source: User Clients
    target: Load Balancer
    protocol: https
    port: 443
  - source: Load Balancer
    target: User Directory Service
    protocol: http
    port: 3000
  - source: User Directory Service
    target: App Database
    protocol: mysql
    port: 3306
